# Feature: VS Code Adapter — Realtime Integration

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-008  
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_websocket_sse.md`

Wire the `IRealtimeAdapter` interface into the VS Code platform adapter so that the WS/SSE UI components built in FEAT-006 and FEAT-007 work end-to-end inside VS Code. This feature implements the two-layer bridge:

1. **Extension host side** (`MessageHandler.ts`) — receives `ws.*` / `sse.*` postMessages from the webview, delegates to the already-implemented `WebSocketService` and `SseService` (FEAT-004, FEAT-005), and pushes back per-connection lifecycle events (`ws.message`, `ws.status`, `ws.headers`, `ws.error`, `sse.event`, `sse.status`, `sse.headers`, `sse.error`) without a `requestId`.

2. **Webview side** (`vsCodeAdapter.ts`) — implements `IRealtimeAdapter` by maintaining an in-memory handle registry, posting `ws.*`/`sse.*` commands to the extension host, and routing incoming push events from the extension host to the correct handle's registered callbacks.

When this feature lands, `adapter.realtime` will no longer be `undefined` in the VS Code platform, and the `useWsConnection` / `useSseConnection` hooks will activate fully.

---

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

No new persistent storage. All new state is **in-memory only**.

### Existing Types Used (no changes)

| Type | Source | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `WsConnectionConfig` | `packages/core/src/types/realtime.ts` | Passed in `ws.connect` postMessage to extension host |
| `WsConnectionHandle` | `packages/core/src/types/realtime.ts` | Returned synchronously by `connectWebSocket()` |
| `SseConnectionConfig` | `packages/core/src/types/realtime.ts` | Passed in `sse.connect` postMessage |
| `SseConnectionHandle` | `packages/core/src/types/realtime.ts` | Returned synchronously by `connectSse()` |
| `WsMessage` | `packages/core/src/types/realtime.ts` | Payload of `ws.message` push events |
| `SseEvent` | `packages/core/src/types/realtime.ts` | Payload of `sse.event` push events |
| `ConnectionStatus` | `packages/core/src/types/realtime.ts` | Payload of `ws.status` / `sse.status` push events |
| `IRealtimeAdapter` | `packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts` | Interface the new `createVSCodeRealtimeAdapter` must implement |
| `webSocketService` | `packages/shared/src/services/WebSocketService.ts` | Singleton used by MessageHandler |
| `sseService` | `packages/shared/src/services/SseService.ts` | Singleton used by MessageHandler |
| `Result` | `packages/core/src/utils/result.ts` | Return type of disconnect/send operations |

### New In-Memory Structures (webview side, in `createVSCodeAdapter` closure)

```typescript
// Internal handle type: extends the public interface with dispatch methods
// used by the push-event router in handleMessage.
interface WsVSCodeHandle extends WsConnectionHandle {
    dispatchMessage(msg: WsMessage): void;
    dispatchStatus(status: ConnectionStatus): void;
    dispatchError(error: string): void;
    dispatchHeaders(headers: Record<string, string>): void;
}

interface SseVSCodeHandle extends SseConnectionHandle {
    dispatchEvent(event: SseEvent): void;
    dispatchStatus(status: ConnectionStatus): void;
    dispatchError(error: string): void;
    dispatchHeaders(headers: Record<string, string>): void;
}

// Registries shared between the realtime adapter and the push-event router
const wsHandles = new Map<string, WsVSCodeHandle>();   // keyed by connectionId
const sseHandles = new Map<string, SseVSCodeHandle>(); // keyed by connectionId
```

### Message Protocol (webview ↔ extension host)

#### Webview → Extension Host (commands)

| Message type | Payload fields | Expected reply |
|---|---|---|
| `ws.connect` | `config: WsConnectionConfig` | Push events (no reply) |
| `ws.disconnect` | `requestId`, `connectionId: string` | `ws.disconnectResponse` (with `requestId`) |
| `ws.send` | `requestId`, `connectionId: string`, `message: string` | `ws.sendResponse` (with `requestId`) |
| `sse.connect` | `config: SseConnectionConfig` | Push events (no reply) |
| `sse.disconnect` | `requestId`, `connectionId: string` | `sse.disconnectResponse` (with `requestId`) |

#### Extension Host → Webview (push events, no `requestId`)

| Message type | Payload fields | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ws.message` | `connectionId`, `message: WsMessage` | Incoming WS message received |
| `ws.status` | `connectionId`, `status: ConnectionStatus` | WS lifecycle transition |
| `ws.headers` | `connectionId`, `headers: Record<string, string>` | WS upgrade response headers |
| `ws.error` | `connectionId`, `error: string` | WS connection error |
| `sse.event` | `connectionId`, `event: SseEvent` | SSE event received |
| `sse.status` | `connectionId`, `status: ConnectionStatus` | SSE lifecycle transition |
| `sse.headers` | `connectionId`, `headers: Record<string, string>` | SSE response headers |
| `sse.error` | `connectionId`, `error: string` | SSE connection error |

## 4. Dependencies

- **FEAT-004** (WebSocket Service) — `webSocketService` singleton in `packages/shared` must exist and export `connect`, `disconnect`, `sendMessage`. ✅ Complete.
- **FEAT-005** (SSE Service) — `sseService` singleton must exist and export `connect`, `disconnect`. ✅ Complete.
- **FEAT-006** (WebSocket UI) — `useWsConnection` hook calls `adapter.realtime.connectWebSocket`, `.disconnectWebSocket`, `.sendWebSocketMessage`. ✅ Complete (guarded against undefined `adapter.realtime`).
- **FEAT-007** (SSE UI) — `useSseConnection` hook calls `adapter.realtime.connectSse`, `.disconnectSse`. ✅ Complete (guarded against undefined `adapter.realtime`).

---

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Extension Host — WebSocket Message Handlers

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `MessageHandler.ts`:
  1. Add `private wsHandles = new Map<string, WsConnectionHandle>()` field.
  2. Add `case 'ws.connect'` in `handleMessage`: extract `config` from `message.config`; call `webSocketService.connect(config)` (returns a `WsConnectionHandle`) — note this is **not awaited**, `connect()` is synchronous; store the handle in `this.wsHandles`; register four callbacks on the handle:
     - `handle.onStatusChange((status) => this.postMessage({ type: 'ws.status', connectionId: config.id, status }))`
     - `handle.onMessage((msg) => this.postMessage({ type: 'ws.message', connectionId: config.id, message: msg }))`
     - `handle.onHeaders((headers) => this.postMessage({ type: 'ws.headers', connectionId: config.id, headers }))`
     - `handle.onError((error) => this.postMessage({ type: 'ws.error', connectionId: config.id, error }))`
  
     **Important**: Store the unsubscribe functions so they can be called on cleanup. Use a `Map<string, Array<() => void>>` (e.g., `wsUnsubscribers`) keyed by `connectionId`.

  3. Add `case 'ws.disconnect'`: extract `connectionId` and `requestId`; call `webSocketService.disconnect(connectionId)` (returns `Promise<Result<void, string>>`); await it; reply: `this.postMessage({ type: 'ws.disconnectResponse', requestId, error: result.isOk ? undefined : result.error })`. Unsubscribe and remove from `this.wsHandles`.

  4. Add `case 'ws.send'`: extract `connectionId`, `message`, `requestId`; call `webSocketService.sendMessage(connectionId, message)`; await it; reply: `this.postMessage({ type: 'ws.sendResponse', requestId, error: result.isOk ? undefined : result.error })`.

  5. On panel dispose (the destructor or wherever the webview panel `onDidDispose` event is currently handled), iterate `this.wsHandles`, call disconnect on each, and invoke all unsubscribers. Examine if `MessageHandler` exposes a `dispose()` method; if not, add one and call it from `extension.ts` on `panel.onDidDispose`.

  6. Add import: `import { webSocketService } from '@wave-client/shared';` at the top of `MessageHandler.ts` alongside the existing shared service imports.

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (`packages/vscode/src/test/handlers/MessageHandlerRealtime.test.ts`):
  - Mock `webSocketService.connect` to return a mock `WsConnectionHandle`; verify `ws.message` is pushed to webview when the mock fires `onMessage`.
  - Verify `ws.status` is pushed on status transitions.
  - Verify `ws.disconnect` handler calls `webSocketService.disconnect` and sends `ws.disconnectResponse` with the correct `requestId`.
  - Verify `ws.send` handler calls `webSocketService.sendMessage` and sends `ws.sendResponse`.
  - Verify that `handleMessage` logs and ignores unknown `ws.connect` messages with malformed config.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add JSDoc comments to each new handler method with `@param` and `@returns`. Add inline comment explaining the unsubscribe cleanup strategy.

- **Verify**: Call `ws.connect` via a mock `handleMessage`, then simulate `ws.disconnect` — both `webSocketService.connect` and `.disconnect` should be called with the right arguments. Existing HTTP tests must still pass.

### TASK-002: Extension Host — SSE Message Handlers

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `MessageHandler.ts`:
  1. Add `private sseHandles = new Map<string, SseConnectionHandle>()` and `private sseUnsubscribers = new Map<string, Array<() => void>>()`.
  2. Add `case 'sse.connect'`: call `sseService.connect(config)` (synchronous, returns `SseConnectionHandle`); store in `this.sseHandles`; register:
     - `handle.onStatusChange((status) => this.postMessage({ type: 'sse.status', connectionId: config.id, status }))`
     - `handle.onEvent((event) => this.postMessage({ type: 'sse.event', connectionId: config.id, event }))`
     - `handle.onHeaders((headers) => this.postMessage({ type: 'sse.headers', connectionId: config.id, headers }))`
     - `handle.onError((error) => this.postMessage({ type: 'sse.error', connectionId: config.id, error }))`
  3. Add `case 'sse.disconnect'`: await `sseService.disconnect(connectionId)`; reply `sse.disconnectResponse` with `requestId`; clean up subscriptions.
  4. Add `sseService` to the existing shared import statement.
  5. On dispose, iterate all SSE handles, disconnect, and invoke unsubscribers.

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (`packages/vscode/src/test/handlers/MessageHandlerRealtime.test.ts`):
  - Mock `sseService.connect` returning a mock `SseConnectionHandle`; verify `sse.event` is pushed when mock fires `onEvent`.
  - Verify `sse.status` is pushed on status change.
  - Verify `sse.disconnect` handler calls `sseService.disconnect` and sends `sse.disconnectResponse`.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on each new SSE method; inline comment explaining SSE handles management.

- **Verify**: `sse.connect` then `sse.disconnect` test cycle passes; no regressions in HTTP or WS handlers.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001 (establishes the pattern; both can be written in the same file edit)

### TASK-003: Webview — VS Code Realtime Adapter (WebSocket Side)

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeAdapter.ts`:

  1. Add imports for `WsConnectionConfig`, `WsConnectionHandle`, `SseConnectionConfig`, `SseConnectionHandle`, `WsMessage`, `SseEvent`, `ConnectionStatus`, `IRealtimeAdapter` at the top.

  2. Define internal interfaces in the file (not exported):
     ```typescript
     interface WsVSCodeHandle extends WsConnectionHandle {
         dispatchMessage(msg: WsMessage): void;
         dispatchStatus(status: ConnectionStatus): void;
         dispatchError(error: string): void;
         dispatchHeaders(headers: Record<string, string>): void;
     }
     ```

  3. Create a `createWsVSCodeHandle(connectionId: string): WsVSCodeHandle` factory function:
     - Each `on*` method pushes `cb` into a `Set<(arg) => void>` and returns an `Unsubscribe` that deletes it from the set.
     - Each `dispatch*` method iterates the corresponding set and calls all callbacks.
     - This is the standard "event emitter in 15 lines" pattern.

  4. In `createVSCodeAdapter`, declare:
     ```typescript
     const wsHandles = new Map<string, WsVSCodeHandle>();
     const sseHandles = new Map<string, SseVSCodeHandle>();
     ```
     These maps are in the factory closure so they are accessible from both the realtime adapter AND the message router.

  5. Create `createVSCodeRealtimeAdapter(wsHandles, sseHandles, vsCodeApi, pendingRequests, defaultTimeout)` function returning `IRealtimeAdapter`:
     ```typescript
     connectWebSocket(config: WsConnectionConfig): WsConnectionHandle {
         const handle = createWsVSCodeHandle(config.id);
         wsHandles.set(config.id, handle);
         vsCodeApi.postMessage({ type: 'ws.connect', config });
         return handle;
     },
     async disconnectWebSocket(connectionId: string): Promise<Result<void, string>> {
         const result = await sendAndWait<void>('ws.disconnect', { connectionId });
         wsHandles.delete(connectionId);
         return result;
     },
     async sendWebSocketMessage(connectionId: string, message: string): Promise<Result<void, string>> {
         return sendAndWait<void>('ws.send', { connectionId, message });
     },
     ```
     
     **Note**: `sendAndWait` here must use the existing `responseDataMap` lookup pattern from `createVSCodeStorageAdapter`. Add entries:
     ```typescript
     'ws.disconnect': '',   // void
     'ws.send': '',         // void
     ```
     And use reply types `ws.disconnectResponse` / `ws.sendResponse`.  
     
     **Alternative approach** (cleaner): Pass the existing `sendAndWait` helper via a shared inline closure, or extract it from the storage adapter factory into the outer `createVSCodeAdapter` scope. Evaluate which is less disruptive to the existing code. If extracting creates excessive churn, a local `sendAndWait` in the realtime adapter factory is acceptable.

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (`packages/vscode/src/test/adapters/vsCodeRealtimeAdapter.test.ts`):
  - Create mock `vsCodeApi` with `postMessage = vi.fn()`.
  - Call `adapter.realtime!.connectWebSocket(config)` — verify `postMessage` called with `{ type: 'ws.connect', config }`.
  - Obtain the handle; register `onMessage` callback; simulate `ws.message` push event via `handleMessage`; verify callback was invoked with the correct `WsMessage`.
  - Simulate `ws.status` push event (status = `'connected'`); verify `onStatusChange` callback fires.
  - Call `adapter.realtime!.disconnectWebSocket(connectionId)` — simulate `ws.disconnectResponse` returning; verify `sendAndWait` resolves to `ok(undefined)`.
  - Call `adapter.realtime!.sendWebSocketMessage(connectionId, 'hello')` — verify `ws.send` posted and resolves to `ok(undefined)` after response.
  - Verify that after `disconnectWebSocket`, calling `connect` again with the same `id` creates a fresh handle.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on `createVSCodeRealtimeAdapter`, `createWsVSCodeHandle`, each method.

- **Verify**: All new tests pass; no TypeScript errors; existing `vsCodeAdapter` tests pass.

### TASK-004: Webview — VS Code Realtime Adapter (SSE Side)

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeAdapter.ts`:

  1. Define `SseVSCodeHandle` (mirrors `WsVSCodeHandle`):
     ```typescript
     interface SseVSCodeHandle extends SseConnectionHandle {
         dispatchEvent(event: SseEvent): void;
         dispatchStatus(status: ConnectionStatus): void;
         dispatchError(error: string): void;
         dispatchHeaders(headers: Record<string, string>): void;
     }
     ```

  2. Create `createSseVSCodeHandle(connectionId: string): SseVSCodeHandle` factory (same pattern as WS).

  3. Add SSE methods to `createVSCodeRealtimeAdapter`:
     ```typescript
     connectSse(config: SseConnectionConfig): SseConnectionHandle {
         const handle = createSseVSCodeHandle(config.id);
         sseHandles.set(config.id, handle);
         vsCodeApi.postMessage({ type: 'sse.connect', config });
         return handle;
     },
     async disconnectSse(connectionId: string): Promise<Result<void, string>> {
         const result = await sendAndWait<void>('sse.disconnect', { connectionId });
         sseHandles.delete(connectionId);
         return result;
     },
     ```
  
  4. Add `'sse.disconnect': ''` to the `responseDataMap`.

  - `connectSse` posts `{ type: 'sse.connect', config }`.
  - `sse.event` push event routes to `onEvent` callback on the right handle.
  - `sse.status` / `sse.headers` / `sse.error` push events route correctly.
  - `disconnectSse` resolves to `ok(undefined)` after `sse.disconnectResponse`.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on `createSseVSCodeHandle` and SSE adapter methods.

- **Verify**: All SSE tests pass; WS tests unaffected.

### TASK-005: Webview — Push Event Routing for WS/SSE in `handleMessage`

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `createVSCodeAdapter` → `handleMessage`:

  In the step-3 push-event `switch`, add the following cases **before** the existing `bannerSuccess` / `bannerError` cases (no functional difference in order, but logically grouped):

  ```typescript
  // ── WebSocket push events ────────────────────────────────────────────
  case 'ws.message': {
      const handle = wsHandles.get(message.connectionId);
      handle?.dispatchMessage(message.message as WsMessage);
      break;
  }
  case 'ws.status': {
      const handle = wsHandles.get(message.connectionId);
      handle?.dispatchStatus(message.status as ConnectionStatus);
      break;
  }
  case 'ws.headers': {
      const handle = wsHandles.get(message.connectionId);
      handle?.dispatchHeaders(message.headers as Record<string, string>);
      break;
  }
  case 'ws.error': {
      const handle = wsHandles.get(message.connectionId);
      handle?.dispatchError(message.error as string);
      break;
  }
  // ── SSE push events ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
  case 'sse.event': {
      const handle = sseHandles.get(message.connectionId);
      handle?.dispatchEvent(message.event as SseEvent);
      break;
  }
  case 'sse.status': {
      const handle = sseHandles.get(message.connectionId);
      handle?.dispatchStatus(message.status as ConnectionStatus);
      break;
  }
  case 'sse.headers': {
      const handle = sseHandles.get(message.connectionId);
      handle?.dispatchHeaders(message.headers as Record<string, string>);
      break;
  }
  case 'sse.error': {
      const handle = sseHandles.get(message.connectionId);
      handle?.dispatchError(message.error as string);
      break;
  }
  ```

  **Note**: WS/SSE push events have no `requestId`, so they reach the switch block. The `message.connectionId` lookup into the maps ties them to the right handle. A missing handle (e.g., stale event after disconnect) is silently ignored via optional chaining — no error is thrown.

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (covered by TASK-003/TASK-004 tests — the tests simulate push events via `handleMessage` which exercises this routing):
  - Verify stale `ws.message` (handle no longer in map) is silently ignored — no throw.
  - Verify two concurrently active WS handles each receive only their own events.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add a comment block above the new switch cases explaining the routing strategy and lifecycle of the handle maps.

- **Verify**: Push events route correctly in the existing unit tests from TASK-003/TASK-004.
- **Depends on**: TASK-003, TASK-004

### TASK-006: Wire Realtime Adapter into `IPlatformAdapter` + Cleanup

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeAdapter.ts`:

  1. At the bottom of `createVSCodeAdapter`, after all other adapter creations:
     ```typescript
     const realtime = createVSCodeRealtimeAdapter(
         wsHandles, sseHandles, vsCodeApi, pendingRequests, defaultTimeout
     );
     ```
     (Or inline directly into the adapter object if the factory signature is too verbose.)

  2. Add `realtime` to the returned `adapter` object:
     ```typescript
     const adapter: IPlatformAdapter = {
         platform: 'vscode',
         storage,
         http,
         file,
         secret,
         security,
         notification,
         arena,
         clipboard,
         realtime,   // ← NEW
         events,
         // ...
     };
     ```

  3. In the `cleanup` function (called by `dispose`), add cleanup for active realtime connections. Iterate `wsHandles` and `sseHandles`, call `webSocketService.disconnect` and `sseService.disconnect` as fire-and-forget, clear the maps:
     ```typescript
     // Note: fire-and-forget on cleanup — we don't await here.
     wsHandles.forEach((_, id) => { void realtime.disconnectWebSocket(id); });
     sseHandles.forEach((_, id) => { void realtime.disconnectSse(id); });
     wsHandles.clear();
     sseHandles.clear();
     ```
     
     **Alternative**: The `IRealtimeAdapter` does not define a `dispose()` method. Keep cleanup in the closure's `cleanup` function rather than adding new interface methods.

  4. In `extension.ts`, verify that `panel.onDidDispose` calls `MessageHandler.dispose()`. If `MessageHandler` does not yet have a `dispose()` method, add one that calls disconnect on all active WS/SSE handles (TASK-001 prepared these cleanup paths).

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (add to `vsCodeRealtimeAdapter.test.ts`):
  - Verify `adapter.realtime` is defined and not `undefined` after `createVSCodeAdapter` returns.
  - Verify calling `adapter.dispose()` clears the handle maps (both `wsHandles` and `sseHandles`).

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Update the file-level JSDoc on `createVSCodeAdapter` to mention the new `realtime` property and the cleanup contract.

- **Verify**: `adapter.realtime` is typed as `IRealtimeAdapter` (non-optional), TypeScript compiles without errors; running the full `pnpm --filter @wave-client/vscode build` succeeds.
- **Depends on**: TASK-003, TASK-004, TASK-005

---

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: Auth resolution (`WsConnectionConfig.auth`, `SseConnectionConfig.auth`) is entirely handled inside `WebSocketService.connect` and `SseService.connect` on the extension host — both already implement auth header injection (FEAT-004/005). No auth logic needed in this adapter.

- **Error Handling**: 
  - `ws.connect` / `sse.connect` are fire-and-forget (no requestId reply). Errors surface via the `ws.error` / `sse.error` push events which the hook routes to `setRealtimeError`. Do not throw from the `case 'ws.connect'` branch on the extension host; always push `ws.status = 'error'` and `ws.error` if `webSocketService.connect` throws synchronously (unlikely since connect() is synchronous and errors surface via `onError` callback, but guard anyway in a try/catch).
  - `ws.disconnect` / `ws.send` / `sse.disconnect` use `sendAndWait` with the standard requestId correlation; errors are returned as `err(message)` via the Result pattern.
  - Stale push events (connectionId not found in the handle maps) are silently ignored — no error notifications.

- **Logging**: 
  - Log connection attempts and disconnects in `MessageHandler` at `console.info` level with `connectionId` and URL (no auth tokens): e.g., `[MessageHandler] WS connect: ${connectionId} → ${url}`.
  - Log push errors at `console.warn` for `ws.error` / `sse.error`: e.g., `[MessageHandler] WS error on ${connectionId}: ${error}`.
  - Log `ws.send` failures at `console.warn`. Do not log message content (could be high volume / sensitive).
  - Webview side: no additional logging needed (the hooks already log via `notification.showNotification`).

- **Security**:
  - Validate WS URL scheme (`ws://` / `wss://`) on the **extension host side** inside the `ws.connect` handler before calling `webSocketService.connect`. If validation fails, push back `ws.error` with a clear message instead of reaching the service (defence-in-depth — the service already validates, but the adapter layer should not forward malformed configs).
  - Do not log full WS/SSE configs (may contain auth tokens).
  - SSE URLs must use `http://` or `https://` — the `SseService` already enforces this, but the handler can early-exit on obvious violations to avoid unnecessary processing.

---
